welcome.simpleMeaning: Hello! Come in and feel happy here
welcome.contextMeaning: A friendly hello to people coming to the school

ovi.simpleMeaning: A name for a person or place
ovi.contextMeaning: The name of the school in this sentence

english.simpleMeaning: The language people speak in England
english.contextMeaning: The language taught at this school

school.contextMeaning: A place where people learn English

today.contextMeaning: On this day, not another day
today.sentence: Today, Emma is in a big mall.

emma.sentence: Today, Emma is in a big mall.

big.sentence: Today, Emma is in a big mall.

mall.simpleMeaning: A place with many shops inside
mall.contextMeaning: A big place with many stores
mall.sentence: Today, Emma is in a big mall.

needs.sentence: She needs lunch, groceries, and a way to pay.

lunch.simpleMeaning: Food you eat in the middle of the day
lunch.contextMeaning: The meal she wants to eat
lunch.sentence: She needs lunch, groceries, and a way to pay.

groceries.simpleMeaning: Food and things you buy for home
groceries.contextMeaning: Food and home items she wants to buy

way.simpleMeaning: A path or method to do something
way.sentence: She needs lunch, groceries, and a way to pay.

pay.contextMeaning: Give money for what she buys
pay.sentence: She needs lunch, groceries, and a way to pay.

feels.contextMeaning: She has the feeling of being a little lost
feels.sentence: She feels a little lost, but she keeps asking.

little.sentence: She feels a little lost, but she keeps asking.

lost.contextMeaning: She feels unsure and confused, but still asks for help
lost.sentence: She feels a little lost, but she keeps asking.

keeps.sentence: She feels a little lost, but she keeps asking.

asking.simpleMeaning: Saying a question to get help
asking.contextMeaning: She is asking questions to learn more
asking.sentence: She feels a little lost, but she keeps asking.

listen.simpleMeaning: Use your ears to hear carefully
listen.contextMeaning: Pay attention to hear the clue
listen.sentence: Listen for one simple clue at each stop.

one.contextMeaning: Just a single clue is needed
one.sentence: Listen for one simple clue at each stop.

simple.contextMeaning: The clue is easy to understand
simple.sentence: Listen for one simple clue at each stop.

clue.simpleMeaning: A small hint that helps you know
clue.contextMeaning: A small hint to help you find the answer
clue.sentence: Listen for one simple clue at each stop.

each.sentence: Listen for one simple clue at each stop.

stop.simpleMeaning: A place where you pause or stay
stop.contextMeaning: A place along the way where you pause
stop.sentence: Listen for one simple clue at each stop.

follow.contextMeaning: Go after her from one place to another
follow.sentence: Can you follow her from the information desk to the checkout?

information.contextMeaning: Helpful facts about where to go
information.sentence: Can you follow her from the information desk to the checkout?

desk.contextMeaning: The place where a worker helps people
desk.sentence: Can you follow her from the information desk to the checkout?

checkout.simpleMeaning: The place where you pay and leave
checkout.contextMeaning: The place where you pay for your things
checkout.sentence: Can you follow her from the information desk to the checkout?

excuse.simpleMeaning: A polite word to get attention
excuse.contextMeaning: A polite way to ask a stranger for help
excuse.sentence: A: Excuse me, where is the food court?

where.contextMeaning: Asking about the place of the food court
where.sentence: A: Excuse me, where is the food court?

food.contextMeaning: Meals or snacks you can buy and eat there
food.sentence: A: Excuse me, where is the food court?

court.simpleMeaning: A place for many shops or places
court.contextMeaning: An area in a mall with many places to eat
court.sentence: A: Excuse me, where is the food court?

go.simpleMeaning: Move from one place to another
go.contextMeaning: Move to the information desk now
go.sentence: B: Go to the information desk first.

first.contextMeaning: Before doing other things, go there
first.sentence: B: Go to the information desk first.

near.contextMeaning: Not far from the main entrance
near.sentence: B: It is near the main entrance.

main.contextMeaning: The biggest entrance people use most
main.sentence: B: It is near the main entrance.

entrance.contextMeaning: The door or opening where people enter
entrance.sentence: B: It is near the main entrance.

what.contextMeaning: Asking about the next thing to do or say

take.contextMeaning: Use the elevator to go up
take.sentence: B: Take the elevator to the third floor.

elevator.simpleMeaning: A box that moves people up and down
elevator.contextMeaning: The machine that carries people to another floor
elevator.sentence: B: Take the elevator to the third floor.

third.contextMeaning: The floor after the second one
third.sentence: B: Take the elevator to the third floor.

floor.simpleMeaning: The flat part you walk on
floor.sentence: B: Take the elevator to the third floor.

when.contextMeaning: At the time you come out
when.sentence: B: When you get out, turn left.

get.simpleMeaning: Come to have or receive something
get.contextMeaning: Come out of a place; also receive the drink
get.sentence: B: When you get out, turn left.

turn.simpleMeaning: Move your body or change direction
turn.sentence: B: When you get out, turn left.

left.sentence: B: When you get out, turn left.

after.contextMeaning: Behind the elevator in time or order

next.contextMeaning: Beside the escalator; also the following time
next.sentence: The food court is next to the escalator.

escalator.simpleMeaning: Moving stairs that carry people up or down
escalator.contextMeaning: The moving stairs near the food court
escalator.sentence: The food court is next to the escalator.

find.simpleMeaning: Look and see where something is
find.contextMeaning: Locate it now; also locate milk and bread

hi.sentence: A: Hi, I’d like a menu, please.

like.contextMeaning: Want a menu; also want a drink
like.sentence: A: Hi, I’d like a menu, please.

menu.simpleMeaning: A list of food and drinks
menu.contextMeaning: The list of food and drinks
menu.sentence: A: Hi, I’d like a menu, please.

please.contextMeaning: A polite word for asking for a menu or tea
please.sentence: A: Hi, I’d like a menu, please.

noodles.simpleMeaning: Long soft food made from flour
noodles.contextMeaning: A kind of food you can eat in a meal
noodles.sentence: We have noodles, sandwiches, and soup.

sandwiches.contextMeaning: Food made with bread and something in the middle
sandwiches.sentence: We have noodles, sandwiches, and soup.

soup.simpleMeaning: Warm liquid food in a bowl
soup.contextMeaning: A hot food you eat with a spoon
soup.sentence: We have noodles, sandwiches, and soup.

want.simpleMeaning: To like something and ask for it
want.contextMeaning: A person says they would like that food
want.sentence: A: I want the chicken noodles.

chicken.simpleMeaning: A white meat from a bird
chicken.contextMeaning: The noodles have meat from a chicken
chicken.sentence: A: I want the chicken noodles.

drink.contextMeaning: A liquid to have with the meal
drink.sentence: B: Would you like a drink with that?

iced.simpleMeaning: Very cold, with ice in it
iced.contextMeaning: Tea that is cold and has ice

tea.simpleMeaning: A hot or cold drink from leaves
tea.contextMeaning: A drink made from tea leaves

combo.simpleMeaning: Two or more foods sold together
combo.contextMeaning: A meal deal with foods together for one price
combo.sentence: B: We have a combo today.

dollars.contextMeaning: The price is 8 units of money

how.simpleMeaning: Asking about the way something happens
how.contextMeaning: Asking about the price of the noodles
how.sentence: A: How much is the noodles alone?

much.simpleMeaning: A question for amount or price
much.sentence: A: How much is the noodles alone?

alone.simpleMeaning: Only this one, not with others
alone.contextMeaning: Just the noodles, not the meal deal
alone.sentence: A: How much is the noodles alone?

saves.contextMeaning: Costs less money than the other choice
saves.sentence: A: So the combo saves 1 dollar.

dollar.sentence: A: So the combo saves 1 dollar.

ll.sentence: A: Okay, I’ll take the combo.

ready.contextMeaning: It will be all set soon

milk.contextMeaning: A white drink in the cold area
milk.sentence: A: Excuse me, where can I find milk and bread?

bread.sentence: A: Excuse me, where can I find milk and bread?

items.sentence: A: Yes, but where are those items?

cold.sentence: B: Milk is in the cold section, aisle 4.

section.sentence: B: Milk is in the cold section, aisle 4.

aisle.sentence: B: Milk is in the cold section, aisle 4.

bakery.contextMeaning: The bread area in the store
bakery.sentence: B: Bread is in the bakery section, near aisle 2.

bananas.sentence: A: Do you have bananas and eggs too?

eggs.contextMeaning: Food from chickens they may have
eggs.sentence: A: Do you have bananas and eggs too?

cheap.contextMeaning: The bananas cost little money today
cheap.sentence: A: Are the bananas cheap today?

sale.simpleMeaning: A time when things cost less
sale.contextMeaning: The bananas are cheaper than usual today

basket.contextMeaning: A container to hold the bananas
basket.sentence: I’ll take a basket and get them all.

all.sentence: I’ll take a basket and get them all.

hello.simpleMeaning: A friendly word when you meet someone
hello.sentence: A: Hello, I’m ready to checkout.

bag.simpleMeaning: A thing you carry things in
bag.contextMeaning: A thing to put the shopping in

small.contextMeaning: A little bag, not a big one
small.sentence: A: Yes, I have one small bag.

total.contextMeaning: The full money to pay is 18 dollars and 50 cents
total.sentence: B: Your total is 18 dollars and 50 cents.

cents.simpleMeaning: Very small parts of a dollar
cents.contextMeaning: The small part of the money after dollars
cents.sentence: B: Your total is 18 dollars and 50 cents.

card.simpleMeaning: A small plastic thing for paying
card.contextMeaning: A bank card used to pay money
card.sentence: A: Can I pay by card?

use.contextMeaning: Do the action with the card machine
use.sentence: Please use the card machine here.

machine.simpleMeaning: A tool that does work by itself
machine.contextMeaning: The device that takes card payments
machine.sentence: Please use the card machine here.

here.contextMeaning: At this place, not somewhere else
here.sentence: Please use the card machine here.

thank.simpleMeaning: Say you are happy for help
thank.contextMeaning: A is saying thanks to B

receipt.simpleMeaning: A paper that shows what you bought
receipt.contextMeaning: A paper from the store for your things

nice.contextMeaning: A friendly wish for a good day

asked.simpleMeaning: Wanted to know or get something
asked.sentence: Today, Emma asked for help, found lunch, bought groceries, and paid at checkout.

help.contextMeaning: Emma wanted someone to assist her
help.sentence: Today, Emma asked for help, found lunch, bought groceries, and paid at checkout.

found.contextMeaning: Emma got lunch after looking for it
found.sentence: Today, Emma asked for help, found lunch, bought groceries, and paid at checkout.

bought.contextMeaning: Emma got groceries by paying money
bought.sentence: Today, Emma asked for help, found lunch, bought groceries, and paid at checkout.

paid.contextMeaning: Emma gave money at the store counter
paid.sentence: Today, Emma asked for help, found lunch, bought groceries, and paid at checkout.

heard.contextMeaning: You listened to those mall words
heard.sentence: You heard useful mall words like elevator, aisle, combo, and receipt.

useful.contextMeaning: These mall words can help you
useful.sentence: You heard useful mall words like elevator, aisle, combo, and receipt.

words.simpleMeaning: Small language pieces we say or write
words.contextMeaning: These are shopping and mall names
words.sentence: You heard useful mall words like elevator, aisle, combo, and receipt.

many.contextMeaning: You can use them in lots of places
many.sentence: You can use these words in many real places.

real.sentence: You can use these words in many real places.

places.contextMeaning: Different locations where you can use them
places.sentence: You can use these words in many real places.

keep.contextMeaning: Continue listening and repeating without stopping
keep.sentence: Keep listening and repeating the short answers aloud.

listening.sentence: Keep listening and repeating the short answers aloud.

repeating.sentence: Keep listening and repeating the short answers aloud.

short.sentence: Keep listening and repeating the short answers aloud.

answers.sentence: Keep listening and repeating the short answers aloud.

aloud.simpleMeaning: So others can hear your voice
aloud.contextMeaning: Say it in a voice people can hear
aloud.sentence: Keep listening and repeating the short answers aloud.

work.sentence: Nice work today, and see you next time.

see.sentence: Nice work today, and see you next time.

time.contextMeaning: Later moment when you meet again
time.sentence: Nice work today, and see you next time.

opening.contextMeaning: The beginning part of the lesson

news.contextMeaning: A label for this speaking part
news.sentence: [NEWS STORY] A: Excuse me, where is the food court?

story.contextMeaning: A label for this speaking part
story.sentence: [NEWS STORY] A: Excuse me, where is the food court?

practice.simpleMeaning: Doing something many times to get better
practice.contextMeaning: A part for learning and trying questions
practice.sentence: [PRACTICE] QUESTION: Where did A go first for help?

question.simpleMeaning: Something you ask to get an answer
question.contextMeaning: The sentence asking where A went first
question.sentence: [PRACTICE] QUESTION: Where did A go first for help?

answer.simpleMeaning: What you say back to a question
answer.contextMeaning: The reply saying A went to the information desk first
answer.sentence: ANSWER: A went to the information desk first.

went.simpleMeaning: Moved from one place to another
went.contextMeaning: Moved to the information desk first
went.sentence: ANSWER: A went to the information desk first.

which.simpleMeaning: Asks you to choose one thing
which.contextMeaning: Asks about the floor B told A
which.sentence: QUESTION: Which floor did B tell A to go to?

tell.contextMeaning: Say to A what floor to go to
tell.sentence: QUESTION: Which floor did B tell A to go to?

said.contextMeaning: Told A to go to the third floor
said.sentence: ANSWER: B said to go to the third floor.

order.contextMeaning: Ask for food at the food court
order.sentence: QUESTION: What did A order at the food court?

ordered.contextMeaning: Asked for chicken noodles and iced tea
ordered.sentence: ANSWER: A ordered chicken noodles and iced tea.

item.contextMeaning: One thing that was on sale
item.sentence: QUESTION: Which item was on sale at the supermarket?

closing.contextMeaning: The end part of the story about Emma
closing.sentence: [CLOSING] Today, Emma asked for help, found lunch, bought groceries, and paid at checkout.
